description: Gateway (lambda-derived) attP1 attachment site; 232 bp, the specificity-1 donor-vector recombination site from pDONR221. The BP reaction (lambda Int + IHF) recombines it only with the cognate attB1 to give attL1 + attR1, enforcing directional recombinational cloning.

references.authors: Hartley JL, Temple GF, Brasch MA
references.title: DNA cloning using in vitro site-specific recombination
Dynamic, structural, and regulatory aspects of lambda site-specific recombination

label: Gateway attP1
note: Gateway recombinational-cloning attP site, specificity 1; 232 bp donor-vector attachment site. Engineered, lambda-derived attP site: it keeps the bacteriophage lambda attP architecture (P and P' arm Int-binding sites and IHF sites flanking the common core) with a central 7 bp overlap modified to set its recombination specificity. The BP reaction (lambda Int + IHF) recombines attB1 with attP1 to give attL1 + attR1. Source: pDONR221 positions 570-801 (+ strand).

label: att core / 7 bp overlap (specificity 1)
note: Common-core crossover region carrying the specificity-1 7 bp overlap (GTACAAAA...); strand exchange by lambda Int occurs across this overlap, and its sequence makes attP1 recombine only with the cognate attB1.

provenance.sequence_source: pDONR221 (Gateway donor vector; SnapGene map in the local sequence store), positions 570-801 (+ strand), 100% identity over 232 bp; byte-verified via tools/source_finder.py --carrier-name pDONR221

functional_claims.label: Gateway attP1 is the donor-vector attachment site for in vitro lambda-integrase (Int + IHF) site-specific recombination; the BP reaction recombines attB1 with attP1 to give attL1 + attR1, capturing an attB1-flanked DNA into the donor backbone as an entry clone.

The attP1 central 7 bp overlap (specificity 1) recombines only with the cognate attB1; this engineered orthogonality between specificity-1 and specificity-2 sites enforces directional, ordered Gateway cloning.

Gateway att sites are engineered derivatives of the bacteriophage lambda att/Int system; attP1 retains the lambda attP arm-type Int sites and IHF sites flanking the common core, with the central overlap altered to set specificity.

Used as the donor (pDONR) site in Gateway cloning: a gene amplified with attB primer tails is captured by BP recombination into a pDONR vector (which carries attP1 and attP2) to make a reusable entry clone.
